name: ExtensionObservation_BodyStructure
title: R5: Observed body structure (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Observation.bodyStructure` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Observation.bodyStructure` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/BodyStructure)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Observation.bodyStructure` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/BodyStructure)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Observation.bodyStructure` has a context of Observation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Observation`.
Element `Observation.bodyStructure` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `Observation.bodyStructure` has a context of Observation based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Observation`.
Element `Observation.bodyStructure` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Only used if not implicit in code found in Observation.code or bodySite is used. In many systems, this may be represented as a related observation instead of an inline component.

element.definition: Indicates the body structure on the subject's body where the observation was made (i.e. the target site).
element.comment: Only used if not implicit in code found in Observation.code or bodySite is used. In many systems, this may be represented as a related observation instead of an inline component.
